mnemonics: My Pet Invites Everyone: MPI_Init, MPI_Finalize, MPI_Send, MPI_Recv - The core MPI party starters.
Cartographers Plot Maps: MPI_Cart_create creates a virtual grid (cartesian topology) for processes.
Rank & Size: Remember Your Social Standing: MPI_Comm_rank (your ID), MPI_Comm_size (total people).
keyPoints: MPI_Init and MPI_Finalize are mandatory functions that bookend any MPI program, initializing and shutting down the MPI environment.
Point-to-point communication is fundamental, using MPI_Send to dispatch a message and MPI_Recv to block until that specific message is received.
Collective operations, like MPI_Bcast (broadcast) and MPI_Reduce, involve all processes in a communicator and are crucial for efficient parallel patterns.
commonMistakes: Deadlock from improper ordering of sends and receives, e.g., having all processes execute a send first, causing them all to wait indefinitely for a corresponding receive that never happens.
Assuming a shared memory space and trying to directly access variables from another process's memory instead of explicitly communicating via MPI_Send/Recv.
Using the default MPI_COMM_WORLD for all operations without creating sub-communicators (e.g., MPI_Comm_split), which can lead to inefficient communication patterns.
